name: New Employee Setup & Account Creation
task:
Reach out to hroperations.support@rescue.org to ensure the new country location and local currency are updated/inputted into the following systems: 
1) HR Operations forms such as RRH, ECR, PTAR; 
2) Anti-Terrorism Check System; and 
3) People Directory and Workday (please note you will be asked to provide an address - this does not need to be specific and can be updated at a later date)

name: R&R Tracker
task: Begin tracking the R&R dates and schedule for deployed and hired staff. Ensure managers have visibility in order to properly plan the re-distribution of work for the needed periods

name: Proceed on initial SRA steps, so to facilitate a more comprehensive Rapid SRA. This need to take place pre-deployment in a desk exercise. Also, check which are the legal requirements for both safety & security in coordination with HR.

name: With the support (when applicable) of other departments, identify operational and logistic requirements. This will ensure that during the first days of the deployment, there will not be surprises.
task: Operational and Logistics requirements

name: Pre-departure briefing including the identified risks, possible access constraints, as well as any gender-sensitive component. Upon finalising this step, staff should sign informed consent forms, including their responsibilities on S&S.
task: Pre-departure briefing

name: A small paragraph (half page maximum), which is a common message of what IRC is, what it is doing in this country/area and what it plans to do in the future. ALL STAFF MUST KNOW AND BE ABLE TO COMMUNICATE.
task: Country's Profile Brief

name:
- All staffs need to submit PoL form to designated focal point
- All national and local staffs need to submit a PoL upon onboarding and before commencing any professional relationship with IRC

name: This form need to be kept at all time during a mission, for liability reasons, as we as for after-mission analysis

name: Taking as consideration the findings of the SRA, budget the security needs and present them to CD for revision. This need to be backed by the SRA as well as the non-security value (recommended add program's value).

name: Mandatory for any Security Level 3 and above, regardless of secured funding
task: Initiate Local Recruitment Process

name: The document have itself guidance and smooth sequence approach. At this stage, paradigm shift of assessment is not mandatory but recommended so to be more flexible and fast, while in the first change, more comprehensive assessments needs to be conducted. Ensure that during the process, you reflect the local legal requirements, both for safety and security.
task: Security Risk Assessment Review

task: Road Traffic Accident Protocols

name: In conjuction with Medical Facilities and Medical Emergency (shall threat is in high risk level), approach this threat. This should be attemted to mitigated both from likelihood and impact, in both proactive and reactive approach. Although IRC has no general SOPs/CPs, specific SOPs/CPs per disease might be available. Contact your RSSD for more information.
task: Proceed on comprehensive assessment on Illness diseases.

name: HEAT training is preferred but an in-person delivery of a relevant training is acceptable.
